tldr: TKC is a CPA-centric Japanese accounting/ERP suite; Avalara is a global subscription tax compliance platform — migrate when JP-only, CPA-driven workflows no longer fit a multi-country, API-first business.
why_switch: Need multi-country sales tax / VAT / GST coverage, not just JP e-tax filing
Want a developer-friendly, API-first compliance platform with 1200+ integrations
Reduce dependence on a CPA-bound, traditional UX
Scale invoicing volume across many jurisdictions
what_to_export: Customer master with tax IDs (JP corporate numbers, foreign VAT IDs)
JP e-tax filing history and acknowledgements
export_method: TKC exports typically go through your CPA's TKC system; coordinate with them and TKC support to extract customer, transaction, and filing data in CSV or system-supported formats. Archive JP e-tax filing acknowledgements separately for audit. Rely on TKC's official guidance rather than assumed APIs, as developer-grade exports are limited.
import_method: Avalara has documented import paths (CSV templates, API, and ERP connectors) for customers, products, transactions, and exemption certificates. Map TKC entities and tax categories to Avalara concepts and import in batches. For ongoing flows, integrate via the AvaTax API or a supported ERP connector; consult Avalara's official documentation for current import templates and API surfaces.

concept_mapping.note: Re-declare nexus per jurisdiction in Avalara.
Manual remapping; JP categories are a subset of AvaTax codes.
Carry over JP corporate numbers and foreign VAT IDs.
Backfill only what you need for audit and reporting.
Confirm JP filing scope; you may still need a JP-local solution.

concept_mapping.target: Avalara ERP connector or AvaTax API
concept_mapping.note: Use a supported connector where possible.

gotchas: JP e-tax filing may still need a JP-local provider — verify Avalara's JP coverage
Tax categories and exemption logic are not 1:1; expect manual mapping
CPA workflows in TKC do not have a direct analog in Avalara
Run parallel for at least one filing cycle to catch discrepancies
Some TKC data may only be exportable via your CPA, not directly
Validate features and endpoints against Avalara's official documentation

email.subject: Migration plan: TKC to Avalara for multi-country tax
email.body:
Hi team,

As our footprint moves beyond Japan, TKC's CPA-centric model is no longer a fit. We're planning a migration to Avalara to get multi-country sales tax, VAT, and GST coverage with an API-first integration story and broad ERP connectors.

We'll coordinate with our CPA to export customers, transactions, and filing acknowledgements from TKC, then map JP tax categories to AvaTax codes and import via Avalara's CSV/API tooling. JP e-tax filing scope will be validated separately — we may keep a JP-local filer alongside Avalara if needed.

Realistic effort is 4-10 weeks with one parallel filing cycle. I'll confirm Avalara's JP coverage and connector options before locking the plan.

Thanks
